sam.subtitle: Outsourced / agency-supplied portion — what we credibly compete for.
filters: Outsourced (not in-house consultancy or developer-employed) portion

stacks.name: Y3 deployment at planning anchor 250
stacks.result_label: S$17.8M Y3 revenue · S$25M Y4 run-rate

implications.headline: Headcount, not contracts, is the binding constraint
implications.body: The market's ceiling is talent supply (~2,400 active C&S RE pool, ~6,800 active C&S RTO pool per IES/ACES JAC register, with the deployable subset materially smaller). Pricing power follows from consistently fielding accredited supervisors when clients need them, not from contract win velocity.

implications.headline: Salary inflation is the dominant cost trend
implications.body: RTO base salaries moved from S$3,800–4,500 (2022) to S$4,800–6,000 (May 2026). 1 July 2026 LQS hike to S$1,800; 1 Jan 2027 EP min to S$6,000; S Pass general min to S$3,600. Margin sensitivity is dominated by base salary and overhead absorption, not billing rate.

implications.headline: 60/40 local/foreign mix is the operating ceiling
implications.body: BCA permanence rule (30% / 1RE+3RTO permanent for 12+ months) caps the gross-margin upside from foreign sourcing. 80/20 local Y1 → 55/45 mature blend per v4. Don't model upside on aggressive foreign mix.

implications.headline: Specialist competency unlocks 20–35% premium
implications.body: PPVC, MET, advanced steel, complex façade and datacentre projects pay 20–35% above standard rates. Adept Academy specialist tracks (developing from Y2) become a direct revenue lever. Public-sector tenders increasingly favour specialist accreditation.

implications.headline: Channel-partner economics validated — payroll-services GM materially above v4 blended model (back-tested, not currently live)
implications.body: A prior completed annual framework agreement with a Tier-2 SG consultancy subsidiary (top-tier-firm-drafted Service Agreement, full cycle ending Q4 2024) showed realized gross margin on the payroll-services tier well above v4's modeled 14–19% blended GM. The two are not contradictory: v4 assumed direct-to-developer Tier-3 firm economics (full QP(S) liability, PI insurance, overhead absorption); the channel work was body-shop / payroll-services structure (S Pass supply with statutory pass-through and a fixed payroll-services fee). Both tiers should be offered side-by-side. The realized rate sets a hard FLOOR for direct-to-developer S Pass billing post-2028 licence — Elitez should not undercut its own historical channel rate.

implications.headline: v4 Year-1 ramp-velocity assumption rebases — fresh build, no covered base
implications.body: Earlier read (since revised) suggested v4's 20–30 Y1 deployed target was partially covered by an active channel relationship. CORRECTION: the channel framework is a *prior* completed engagement (Q4 2024) with no current deployment. Year-1 ramp-velocity therefore rebases to v4's original assumption — a fresh build, not an extension of a live book. Operating-track-record claim from the completed engagement remains intact and is a procurement-screening asset; deployment economics for Y1 do not benefit from carryover. Customer-concentration risk also resets to v4 baseline.

implications.headline: Operator non-compete cleared — v4 §13 SJ Commercial Pushback risk softens further
implications.body: Operators recruited from Surbana Jurong are not bound by an SJ non-compete affecting this venture. Combined with the prior commercial supplier relationship (Tier-2 subsidiary as past customer), the SJ-pushback risk in v4 §13 (Low–Med) reduces to Low. Pre-incorporation Day-7 operator non-compete diligence (v4 §14.2 Action #1) can be deprioritised. Continue to manage relationship-rupture risk, which is separate from non-compete enforceability.

market_size.methodology_appendix: TAM = SG 2026 construction contract value × share requiring formal supervision (>S$5M projects) × site supervision spend as % of project value (0.6–1.2%). SAM filters to the outsourced/agency-supplied portion (~35–45% per market interviews + BCA QP(S) tender award triangulation). SOM is Y3 base case (planning anchor 250 EoP, average 150) at S$9,800/mo blended billing.

policies.title: BCA firm-based licensing for site supervision (mandatory 2028)
policies.body: All building projects above S$75M must engage a BCA-licensed Site Supervision Firm from 2028. Three pillars: firm-level systems (ISO/IEC 17020), key personnel (Technical Director PE-Civil + 2 QPs + 30% permanent core), operational/SOP/tech (calibrated equipment, video streaming, 7-yr retention).
policies.implication_for_us: Defines the moat. We file Q3 2027, target licence Q1–Q2 2028 — 6+ months ahead of mandatory.

policies.title: SAC accreditation scheme launch (2026)
policies.body: Singapore Accreditation Council ISO/IEC 17020 accreditation scheme for Site Supervision Firms launches in 2026. Sets the procedural template parallel to the existing Lift & Escalator Certification Firm scheme.
policies.implication_for_us: First-mover firms gain ~12–18 month head start. Apply Month 14 of operation.

policies.title: BCA Price Quality Method (PQM) — environmental sustainability mandatory >S$50M
policies.body: Since 31 Jan 2025, environmental sustainability is a mandatory PQM quality attribute for public-sector construction services tenders >S$50M. EcoVadis, ISO 14001, Sustainability Innovation Award convert directly to scoreable points.
policies.implication_for_us: Elitez Group EcoVadis Silver + CPLC + Champion of Good convert to PQM points. No Tier-3 competitor matches this.

policies.title: Local Qualifying Salary hike — S$1,800 from 1 July 2026
policies.body: From 1 July 2026, locals must earn ≥ S$1,800 to count fully toward foreign-worker quota; S$900–1,799 counts as 0.5. All Elitez Site Supervisor local headcount sits well above S$1,800 — quota effect neutral-to-positive for our footprint.
policies.implication_for_us: Disqualifies low-paid administrative roles from quota math; protects supervisors.

policies.title: S Pass general minimum rises to S$3,600 (1 Jan 2027)
policies.body: S Pass general minimum salary rises to S$3,600 from 1 January 2027 (new applications). EP rises from S$5,600 to S$6,000. S Pass levy harmonised flat S$650/mo (Tier 1) since Sep 2025.
policies.implication_for_us: Compresses foreign-mix margins. Y3 cost build assumes S$3,800 S Pass base premium above min.

policies.title: Construction sector DRC at 83.3% (1:5)
policies.body: MOM construction Dependency Ratio Ceiling is 83.3% (1:5) since January 2024 (was 87.5% / 1:7 prior). S Pass sub-DRC at 15%. The 1:7 ratio cited in earlier proposal drafts was incorrect.
policies.implication_for_us: Tighter than older guidance. Maintain DRC headroom by accelerating SG/PR hiring in Y1 to enable foreign expansion in Y2–3.

policies.title: BCA HR-plan permanence rule — 30% / 12 months
policies.body: Draft requirement: 30% of REs/RTOs (or 1 RE + 3 RTOs minimum) must be permanent staff for ≥ 12 consecutive months. This is implicit protection for firms with retention infrastructure.
policies.implication_for_us: Body shops can't satisfy with rotating headcount. Elitez's long-tenure track record converts to a regulatory asset.

cultural_signals.observation: Public scrutiny of foreign workers in safety-critical inspection roles
cultural_signals.evidence: Recurring local media topic; LTA, HDB tender language increasingly specifies Singaporean/PR composition.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Map foreign supervisors to private-sector projects and to main contractors with mature foreign workforces; document competency and accreditation defensibly.

cultural_signals.observation: Procurement increasingly screens on training capability
cultural_signals.evidence: Public-sector tenders explicitly request HR plans; PQM scoring includes talent-development criteria.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Adept Academy is a procurement-screening winner — not just an internal cost saver.

economic_signals.implication_for_us: Multi-decade high; 2026 forecast S$47–53B; 2027–2030 floor S$39–46B per BCA forecast.
Anchored by Changi T5, Cross Island Line Ph 2/3, Tuas Mega Port, NUH, 55,000 BTO.

economic_signals.indicator: Active C&S RE pool — IES/ACES JAC register
economic_signals.implication_for_us: Deployable subset materially smaller — many retired, on long-term assignments, or in-house at contractors.

economic_signals.indicator: Active C&S RTO pool — IES/ACES JAC register

economic_signals.implication_for_us: Up from S$3,800–4,500 (2022). New entrants increasingly diverted to data centre operations and semiconductor fabs.

adoption_patterns.note: Penetration in this market reads as buyer-side licence-readiness, not 'adoption' in the SaaS sense.

trends.title: Public-sector tenders trending toward larger consolidated packages
trends.evidence: Multi-firm consortium awards (LTA Cross Island Line Ph1 Pkg2 split across 5 firms); package size increasing in line with mega-project pipeline.
trends.implication_for_us: Above-S$75M threshold — only BCA-licensed firms post-2028. Pre-license, consortium-with-RCY is the realistic path for public-sector references.

trends.title: PPVC adoption accelerating with HDB BTO scale
trends.evidence: 55,000-unit BTO programme uses PPVC and prefabrication extensively; PPVC supervision is its own competency tier.
trends.implication_for_us: Specialist competency leadership (PPVC, post-tensioning, structural steel) emerges Year 2 onward — direct premium-pricing lever.

trends.title: Environmental sustainability becoming PQM tender gate
trends.evidence: Mandatory quality attribute since 31 Jan 2025 for tenders >S$50M; ESG credentials directly scoreable.
trends.implication_for_us: Group ESG stack (EcoVadis, CPLC, Champion of Good, ISO 14001 in progress) is the PQM tender advantage no Tier-3 competitor has.

trends.title: WSQ / SCTP / CCP funding compressing reskill cost
trends.evidence: WSQ subsidies 50–90%; SCTP up to 90% salary support; CCP for mid-career converts.
trends.implication_for_us: Adept Academy reskill pipeline is structurally cheaper than competitor recruitment. Revenue secondary; supply security primary.
